originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Our Friend Paul Biya Gets a Helping Hand!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's me, your friendly news reporter for Africa! Today, we have some special news from a beautiful country called Cameroon. It’s a place with lots of sunshine and happy people, just like many places in Africa. We are going to talk about something important happening with their leader. It’s a story about a very old leader getting a new helper.

Who is President Paul Biya?

Imagine someone who has been helping to lead your country for a very, very long time. That’s like President Paul Biya! He is the leader of Cameroon. He is 93 years old. Wow, that’s very old! Maybe even older than your grandpa or great-grandpa! He has been the President for so many years. He started being President even before many of your parents were born! He works to make Cameroon a good place for everyone, especially for kids like you. Did you know? Being a President is a very big job! They help make decisions for the whole country, like building schools, making sure people have food, and keeping everyone safe.

Where is Cameroon?

Cameroon is a lovely country right here in Africa! It is in a part of Africa called Central Africa. It has pretty beaches, tall mountains, and big, green forests. Many different animals live there, too, like elephants and gorillas! People in Cameroon speak different languages, but French and English are very common. It's a country full of life and happy families. Imagine looking at a map of Africa; Cameroon is right there, like a little heart! Did you know? Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different landscapes and cultures, like a little bit of every part of Africa!

What Does a President Do?

So, what does a President actually do? Think of your school principal. They make sure the school is safe, that teachers are happy, and that you learn lots of new things. A President does this for a whole country! President Biya makes sure that Cameroon has good roads, that hospitals are there when people are sick, and that kids can go to school and learn. It's a job where you have to think about a lot of people and what they need every day. Did you know? Presidents meet with leaders from other countries to make friends and help each other. It's like making friends with other schools!

A Very Long Time to Lead

President Paul Biya has been the leader of Cameroon for a super long time – 43 years! Can you imagine that? That's more than half of his life! For 43 years, he has been working for Cameroon. It means he has seen many changes and helped his country grow. It's like having the same head teacher at your school for 43 years! They would know everything about the school and how to make it better. He was chosen to be President again last year. When a country chooses its leader, it's called an election. People vote to pick who they think will be the best leader. Did you know? Some trees in Africa can live for hundreds of years! Just like President Biya has been leading for many, many years.

What is a Deputy?

Now, here's the exciting new part! President Biya is going to get a very important helper called a "deputy." What is a deputy? Imagine you are playing a game with your friends, and you are the team captain. Sometimes, you might need someone to help you make decisions or to take your place if you are not there. That person is like your deputy! A deputy for a President is someone who helps the President with their big job. They learn from the President, help with important tasks, and can even step in if the President is busy or needs a rest. It's like having a co-captain for the country! This person will work very closely with President Biya to make Cameroon even better. Did you know? In some schools, there are "prefects" or "monitors" who help the teachers keep things running smoothly. They are like helpers, just like a deputy!

A New Helper for President Biya!

This is big news because President Biya has not had a deputy like this for 43 years! Yes, for all the time he has been President, he has been doing most of the very big tasks by himself or with other ministers, but not a specific main helper who could step in as President. Now, for the first time in a very long time, he will have this special helper. This new deputy will be a very important person. They will learn all about being President from President Biya. It means there will be two very important people working together to lead Cameroon. This new helper will be there to make sure everything keeps going well, even when President Biya needs to take a break or has other things to do. It’s like getting an extra pair of hands to help carry a very big and important box! Did you know? Teamwork makes big jobs easier! When people work together, they can do amazing things.

Why is Having a Helper Good?

Why is it good to have a deputy? Well, imagine you have a lot of homework to do. If someone helps you, it becomes easier and faster, right? The same goes for leading a country! It's a huge job with many responsibilities. Having a deputy means the work can be shared. It means there will be more people thinking about how to help the country. It also means that someone new can learn all the important things about being a leader. This is great for the future of Cameroon because it helps prepare someone to be a good leader someday. It's like having a teacher who helps a student learn how to become a teacher themselves! More help means more good things can happen for the people of Cameroon. Did you know? Even superheroes have sidekicks to help them save the day! Having a helper is smart!

Did You Know? Fun Facts!

* Cameroon has a mountain called Mount Cameroon, which is a volcano! It sometimes glows red at night, but don't worry, it's usually safe! * Football (soccer) is very popular in Cameroon! They have a famous football team called the "Indomitable Lions." * In Cameroon, children love to play games like "Ampe" (a jumping game) and make toys from recycled materials, just like kids all over Africa! * The capital city of Cameroon is called Yaoundé. It's a busy and exciting city!

What Does This Mean for You?

So, what does this big news mean for kids like you? It means that the leaders of Cameroon are thinking about how to make sure the country stays strong and happy for a long, long time. Having a deputy means more hands to help, more minds to think, and a clearer path for the future. It means that President Biya will have extra support to keep making sure schools are good, hospitals work well, and everyone in Cameroon is safe and cared for. It's all about making sure that Cameroon continues to be a wonderful place to live and grow up! And that is good news for everyone!
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is 93 years old and has been leading for 43 years, is getting a special helper called a deputy for the very first time to help him lead the country.
whyMatters: This is important because it means President Biya will have extra help to lead Cameroon, and it helps prepare someone new to be a leader in the future, making sure the country stays strong and happy.

originalTitle: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
title: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
content: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: Our Homes for Nigeria's Big Capital City!
content:
Hello, Super Friends from Africa!

Hello everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, reporting live from wonderful Africa! Today, we are going to talk about a very special place in Nigeria. Nigeria is a big, beautiful country in West Africa, full of amazing people, animals, and stories. Our story today is about a very important city called Abuja.

What is a Capital City?

Every country has a special city where its leaders work. This special city is called the 'capital city'. It's like the main office for the whole country! In the capital city, big decisions are made to help everyone. The capital city for Nigeria used to be a very busy city called Lagos. But a long time ago, Nigeria decided it needed a new, central capital city.

Building a Brand New City: Abuja!

So, Nigeria chose a quiet place right in the middle of the country to build a brand new capital. They named this new city Abuja! Imagine starting a city from almost nothing. Abuja was planned to be a beautiful, modern city with wide roads, big buildings, and lots of green trees. It was going to be a place where leaders could work well and where everyone would feel welcome. Did you know? Abuja became Nigeria's official capital city in 1991! That's not too long ago!

Homes Had to Move

Now, here's a very important part of our story. Before Abuja was built, many people already lived on that land. They had their homes, their small farms, their schools, and their playgrounds there. This was their community, where their families had lived for a very long time. They loved their homes and their land very much. To build the big new city of Abuja, these people had to move. They had to leave their old homes and their old way of life. When people have to leave their homes so that something new can be built, it is sometimes called 'eviction'. It means they did not choose to leave, but they had to for the new city.

Promises, Promises!

When the government asked these people to move, they made some very important promises. A promise is when someone says they *will* do something for you. The government leaders said they would help these families. They promised to give them new homes. They also promised to help them find new schools for their children. And they promised new places to work or new land for farming so they could still take care of their families. These promises were very important to the people. They trusted the government to help them start new lives after moving from their old homes. They were sad to leave, but the promises gave them hope for a good new beginning.

What Went Wrong?

Now, many years have passed since Abuja was built. Abuja is a beautiful city today! But some of the people who moved from their homes are sad. They are saying that many of the promises made to them were not kept. This means the government did not do all the things they said they would do. Imagine if your friend promised to share their sweets with you, but then they ate all of them! You would feel sad and maybe a little bit let down, right? These people feel like that, but it's about their homes and their whole lives. They are still waiting for the new homes they were promised. Some might still be waiting for good schools for their children or new places to work.

Why Do They Feel Sad?

When promises are broken, it can make people feel very sad and even angry. These people loved their old homes, and they moved because they were told they would get help. They believed in the promises. Now, they feel like their voices are not being heard. They feel like the government has forgotten about them and the help they needed. Did you know? Trust is very important! When people keep their promises, it builds trust. When promises are broken, it can be hard to trust again.

They Want a Voice in Their Land

The people who moved want to tell their story. They want the government to listen to them. This is what they mean by wanting a 'voice' in their land. They want to be heard. They want to remind everyone about the important promises that were made to them many years ago. They believe that since Abuja is a city for *all* Nigerians, it should also be fair to *all* the people, especially those who gave up their homes for it. They are asking for the promises to be fulfilled so they can live happy and safe lives too.

Working Together for a Better Future

It is always best when people can talk together and solve problems. When the government listens to its people, and people share their feelings, good things can happen. It's like when you have a problem with a friend, and you talk about it to make things better. We hope that the leaders in Nigeria will listen to these families. We hope that everyone can work together as a team to make sure these promises are kept. This way, Abuja can truly be a wonderful capital city for *all* of Nigeria's amazing people, and everyone can feel happy and safe in their homes. Thank you for listening to this important story, my friends! See you next time!
summary: People who gave up their homes to build Nigeria's capital city, Abuja, say the government has not kept its promises to give them new homes and help.
whyMatters: It is important because everyone deserves a home, and promises should always be kept so people can trust their leaders and feel safe and happy.

originalTitle: US satellite firm Planet Labs announces blackout on war on Iran images
title: Super Cameras in Space Take a Little Break!
content:
Jambo, my amazing friends across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. Do you ever look up at the big blue sky during the day, or at the sparkling stars at night? Our world is full of wonders, and guess what? Some of the most amazing helpers for our planet are flying very, very high up there, almost kissing the stars!

Meet Our Sky Friends: Satellites!

Imagine you have a super-duper camera that can fly all around our Earth, way, way above the clouds. These super cameras are called satellites! They are like special robots that zoom around our planet every single day. They take millions of pictures of everything below them – from big cities to huge forests, sparkling oceans to wide-open savannas. Did you know? Some satellites are as big as a small bus! And they travel super fast, faster than any car or airplane you've ever seen. They go around our whole Earth in just a few hours!

Planet Labs: The Company with Super Eyes

Now, there's a special company called 'Planet Labs.' Sounds cool, right? This company has lots and lots of these super camera satellites. They are like a big team of space photographers! Their job is to take pictures of our Earth constantly. These pictures help grown-ups learn many things. For example, they can see how farms are growing food, how rivers are flowing, or even how our beautiful wildlife habitats are changing. Did you know? Planet Labs has more than 150 satellites! That's a lot of eyes in the sky, always watching and taking photos to help us understand our home planet better.

A Special Request: Taking a Break from Pictures

But something interesting just happened. Planet Labs, with all their super eyes, has decided to stop showing some of their pictures. Not all of them, just pictures from a special place far away. Imagine if you were taking pictures of a game, and then someone important asked you to keep some of those pictures private for a little while. This happened because grown-ups who lead a country far away asked Planet Labs to do this. They asked them to not share pictures about things happening in a place called Iran. Iran is a country very far from Africa, across oceans and lands.

Why Keep Pictures Secret Sometimes?

You might wonder, 'Why would they do that?' Well, sometimes, grown-ups have very important reasons for keeping certain information private. It's like when you have a surprise party, and you don't want anyone to see the decorations until everything is ready! These grown-ups in charge of countries sometimes need to make sure things are safe and quiet. When big things are happening, they might ask companies like Planet Labs to be extra careful with what pictures they share. It's about being responsible and thinking about everyone's safety. It's a very grown-up decision.

Learning About Our World, Together

Even though we can't see all the pictures from space all the time, we can still learn so much about our amazing world. Satellites help us in many ways, every day! They help us predict the weather, which is great for farmers. They help us understand climate change, which helps us protect our planet. And they help scientists discover new things about Earth. Did you know? Satellites also help our phones work, helping us talk to friends and family far away! So, remember, even when our sky friends, the satellites, take a little break from showing some pictures, they are still up there, working hard for us. It's a big, wide world, full of amazing people and places. And we can always be curious, ask questions, and keep learning new things every day! Keep looking up at the sky, my friends!
summary: A company called Planet Labs, which has many cameras in space called satellites, has stopped showing some of its pictures of a faraway place called Iran because grown-ups in charge of a country asked them to.
whyMatters: These pictures help us learn about our world, but sometimes grown-ups decide to keep some photos private for important reasons, like keeping people safe.

originalTitle: Search for missing airman presents serious test for US
title: Let's Find Our Missing Pilot Friend!
content:
Hello, Brave Listeners!

Hello everyone, my super friends! I'm your news reporter from Africa, here to tell you a very important story. It's a story about helping a friend, even when that friend is far away. We are going to talk about a brave pilot who is missing. This pilot is a very special person. He flies big airplanes high up in the sky. He helps his country, just like a helpful friend helps you. He is called an 'airman' because he spends his time in the air.

What Happened to Our Pilot Friend?

One day, this pilot was flying his big airplane. It was a very big airplane, like a giant bird made of metal! But something happened, and his plane went down. It means the plane could not fly anymore. Imagine if your toy airplane fell down and you couldn't find it. You would feel sad, right? Well, this is a real pilot, and he is a real person. So, many people are feeling sad and worried. The pilot was not alone in the plane. There was another kind friend with him. This other friend also helps fly the plane. Now, both of them are missing. This makes the story even more important.

What Does 'Missing' Mean?

When someone is 'missing', it means we don't know where they are. It means they are lost. Maybe they are somewhere hiding, or maybe they need help to be found. It is a very worrying thing when someone you care about is missing. Think about playing hide-and-seek. When your friend hides very well, and you cannot find them, they are 'missing' from your sight for a little while. But this pilot is missing for real, and he needs help to be found. Did you know? Airplanes can fly faster than a car! They can travel all over the world.

A Big Search is Happening!

Because our pilot friend is missing, many, many people are looking for him. This is called a 'search'. They are looking everywhere! They are looking on the ground, and they are looking from other planes in the sky. It's like when you lose your favorite toy, and all your family helps you look for it. This is a much bigger search! Brave people from his country are trying their very best to find him and his friend. They use special tools to help them look. They use special radar, which is like a magic eye that can see far away. They also use other airplanes to fly low and look very carefully.

Why is it Important to Find Them Quickly?

It is very important to find our pilot friend and his other friend quickly. Imagine being lost all by yourself. You might feel scared or cold. You would want someone to find you fast, wouldn't you? The people looking for him want to make sure he is safe and sound. They want to bring him back to his home and his family. His family misses him very much. His friends miss him too. Did you know? Pilots train for many years to learn how to fly big airplanes safely! They are very clever.

Who Are These Helpers?

The helpers are from the pilot's country. They are like a big family, and when one person is lost, everyone tries to help. They are working very, very hard day and night. They are brave people themselves. They go out to look even when it is difficult. They know that every minute counts when someone is missing. We should clap for these amazing helpers! Some helpers are pilots too, flying other planes to search. Some are on the ground, walking and looking. Some are using computers to help find clues. Everyone is playing a part!

Imagine You Are Helping

Imagine you are part of the search team! What would you do? You would look with your super-sharp eyes. You would listen with your super-hearing ears. You would hope very hard to find them. When we are looking for someone, we always hope for the best. We hope they are safe. We hope they are warm. We hope they are waiting for us to find them. Did you know? Some search teams have special dogs that can smell where people might be! Those dogs are super sniffers!

Sending Our Good Wishes

From Africa, we send our good wishes to the pilot and his friend. We hope they are safe. We hope the helpers find them very, very soon. It shows us that people all over the world care about each other. Even if we don't know them, we can still hope for them. We can still think good thoughts for them. It reminds us that helping others is one of the kindest things we can do. Let's all hope for a happy ending to this story. We want our pilot friend and his friend to be back home with their families soon. It is a big test for all the helpers, but they are doing a fantastic job! Keep searching, brave helpers!
summary: Many people are working together to find a brave pilot and his friend who are lost after their big airplane went down, hoping to bring them home safely.
whyMatters: It matters because it is very important to help someone who is lost, and everyone hopes the pilot and his friend can come home safe to their family and friends.

originalTitle: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
title: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
content: Nobody would have believed that 12 months on from Liverpool's title win, a fanbase so united behind its team and its manager would be, at best, apathetic as to whether Arne Slot remains in the job.
